body:
Title: CRA 2025 T1 total income, net income, and taxable income aggregation.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package/5000-r.html
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5000-r/5000-r-25e.txt

CRA Form 5000-R Income Tax and Benefit Return for 2025 Step 2 calculates total income. Lines 1 to 20 include employment income line 10100, other employment income line 10400, old age security pension line 11300, CPP or QPP benefits line 11400, other pensions and superannuation line 11500, elected split-pension amount line 11600, universal child care benefit line 11700, employment insurance and other benefits line 11900, taxable dividends line 12000, interest and other investment income line 12100, net partnership income line 12200, RDSP income line 12500, net rental income line 12600, taxable capital gains line 12700, taxable support payments received line 12800, RRSP income line 12900, taxable FHSA income line 12905, taxable FHSA income other line 12906, other income line 13000, and taxable scholarships line 13010. Line 21 adds lines 1 to 20.

Step 2 line 27 adds net business income line 13500, net professional income line 13700, net commission income line 13900, net farming income line 14100, and net fishing income line 14300 as net self-employment income. Line 28 adds line 21 and line 27. Line 32 and line 14700 add workers' compensation benefits line 14400, social assistance payments line 14500, and net federal supplements line 14600. Line 33 and line 15000 add line 28 and line 32 as total income.

Step 3 calculates net income. Line 53 and line 23300 add deduction lines 35 to 52, including RPP deduction line 20700, RRSP deduction line 20800, FHSA deduction line 20805, elected split-pension deduction line 21000, union or professional dues line 21200, UCCB repayment line 21300, child care expenses line 21400, disability supports deduction line 21500, allowable business investment loss line 21700, moving expenses line 21900, allowable support payments made line 22000, carrying charges line 22100, CPP or QPP self-employment deduction line 22200, CPP or QPP enhanced employment deduction line 22215, exploration and development expenses line 22400, other employment expenses line 22900, clergy residence deduction line 23100, and other deductions line 23200. Line 54 and line 23400 subtract line 53 from line 34 and may be negative as net income before adjustments. Line 55 and line 23500 is social benefits repayment. Line 56 and line 23600 subtract line 55 from line 54 and floors negative amounts at zero as net income.

Step 4 calculates taxable income. Line 68 and line 25700 add Canadian Armed Forces personnel and police deduction line 24400, security options deductions line 24900, other payments deduction line 25000, limited partnership losses of other years line 25100, non-capital losses of other years line 25200, net capital losses of other years line 25300, qualifying business transfer or cooperative conversion capital gains deduction line 25395, capital gains deduction line 25400, northern residents deductions line 25500, and additional deductions line 25600. Line 69 and line 26000 subtract line 68 from line 57 and floors negative amounts at zero as taxable income.
